description: Move game business logic to NestJS backend, add 2-player PvP mode with WebSocket real-time updates, restrict PostGraphile to read-only, make frontend a thin client

userStories.title: Database schema migration for game mode and turn tracking
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the game table to support game mode, turn tracking, and player identity so the backend can enforce game rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Truncate existing game data (fresh start for dev environment)
New TypeORM migration adds columns: mode (varchar(10), default 'ai'), current_turn (varchar(1), default 'X'), player_x_token (uuid, nullable), player_o_token (uuid, nullable)
game.entity.ts updated with new columns: mode typed as 'ai' | 'pvp', currentTurn typed as 'X' | 'O', playerXToken typed as string | null, playerOToken typed as string | null
Migration runs successfully: pnpm typeorm:migration:run
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Entity file: apps/tictactoe-api/src/modules/game/game.entity.ts. Use SnakeNamingStrategy (camelCase in TS -> snake_case in DB). Truncate via migration up() before ALTER TABLE.

userStories.title: Copy game logic and shared types to backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the pure game logic functions available in the backend so GameService can validate moves and compute state transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/tictactoe-api/src/lib/game/types.ts with shared types: CellValue ('X' | 'O' | null), Board (9-element tuple), GameStatus ('in_progress' | 'x_wins' | 'o_wins' | 'draw'), GameMove ({position, player, moveNumber}), GameMode ('ai' | 'pvp')
Create apps/tictactoe-api/src/lib/game/gameLogic.ts copied verbatim from apps/tictactoe/src/lib/game/gameLogic.ts
Update imports in gameLogic.ts to use co-located types.ts instead of @/types/game
All pure functions available: checkWinner, isBoardFull, getAvailablePositions, getGameStatus, getCurrentTurn, isValidMove, applyMove, createEmptyBoard, formatBoardForDisplay
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: apps/tictactoe/src/lib/game/gameLogic.ts and apps/tictactoe/src/types/game.ts. Functions are pure with zero framework dependencies — only import path changes needed.

userStories.title: Create GameModule with create game REST endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend client, I want to create a new game via POST /api/games so the backend initializes and tracks the game.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/tictactoe-api/src/modules/game/dto/create-game.dto.ts with mode field ('ai' | 'pvp')
Create apps/tictactoe-api/src/modules/game/dto/game-state.dto.ts with fields: id, board, status, winner, moves, mode, currentTurn, createdAt, updatedAt
Create apps/tictactoe-api/src/modules/game/game.service.ts with createGame() method
Create apps/tictactoe-api/src/modules/game/game.controller.ts with POST /api/games endpoint
Create apps/tictactoe-api/src/modules/game/game.module.ts importing TypeOrmModule.forFeature([Game])
Register GameModule in AppModule imports
POST /api/games accepts { mode: 'ai' | 'pvp' } body
Creates game in DB: empty board, status in_progress, current_turn X, mode from request
Generates and stores player_x_token (UUID v4) for the creating player
Returns GameStateDto with game state plus playerToken field
Returns 400 for invalid mode values
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use @Controller('api/games') prefix. Use TypeORM Repository<Game> injected via constructor. Generate UUID with crypto.randomUUID() or uuid package. Do NOT expose player tokens in GameStateDto — return playerToken as a separate top-level field in the response.

userStories.title: Get game state REST endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend client, I want to fetch the current game state via GET /api/games/:id so I can render the board.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/games/:id returns GameStateDto with id, board, status, winner, moves, mode, currentTurn, createdAt, updatedAt
Returns 404 with descriptive message for non-existent game IDs
Does NOT expose playerXToken or playerOToken in the response
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add getGame() method to GameService and @Get(':id') to GameController. Use repository.findOne({ where: { id } }) with NotFoundException.

userStories.title: Make move REST endpoint (core game logic)
userStories.description: As a player, I want to submit a move via POST /api/games/:id/move so the backend validates and applies it.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/tictactoe-api/src/modules/game/dto/make-move.dto.ts with position field (number 0-8)
POST /api/games/:id/move accepts { position: number } body
Loads game from DB using TypeORM query runner with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE for concurrency safety
Rejects with 400 if game is not in_progress
Rejects with 400 if position is invalid via isValidMove(board, position)
Applies move via applyMove(board, position, currentTurn)
Records move in moves array: { position, player: currentTurn, moveNumber }
Checks game status via getGameStatus(newBoard)
If game over: updates status and winner, saves, returns final GameStateDto
If game continues: flips currentTurn (X->O or O->X), saves, returns updated GameStateDto
Returns 404 for non-existent game ID
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use TypeORM EntityManager.transaction() with queryRunner for SELECT ... FOR UPDATE. Import game logic functions from src/lib/game/gameLogic. Board is stored as JSONB — cast to Board type. AI integration and token validation are separate stories.

userStories.title: List games REST endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend client, I want to list recent games via GET /api/games so I can display game history.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/games returns array of GameStateDto ordered by createdAt descending
Supports optional ?limit=N query param (default 20)
Does NOT expose player tokens in response objects
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use repository.find({ order: { createdAt: 'DESC' }, take: limit }). Add @Get() with @Query('limit') param to GameController.

userStories.title: Join PvP game REST endpoint
userStories.description: As Player O, I want to join an existing PvP game via POST /api/games/:id/join so I receive my player token.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/games/:id/join generates and stores player_o_token (UUID v4)
Returns GameStateDto plus playerToken field for Player O
Rejects with 400 if game mode is not pvp
Rejects with 409 Conflict if Player O has already joined (playerOToken is not null)
Returns 404 for non-existent game ID
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add joinGame() to GameService and @Post(':id/join') to GameController.

userStories.title: Migrate AI pipeline to backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the LangGraph AI pipeline running in the NestJS backend so GameService can trigger it after validating a human move.
acceptanceCriteria: Install LangGraph dependencies in tictactoe-api: @langchain/aws, @langchain/core, @langchain/langgraph, zod
Create apps/tictactoe-api/src/modules/ai/ai.module.ts exporting AiModule
Create apps/tictactoe-api/src/modules/ai/ai.service.ts with generateMove(board: Board): Promise<number>
Copy AI files from apps/tictactoe/src/lib/ai/ to apps/tictactoe-api/src/modules/ai/: move-graph.ts, nodes.ts, graph-state.ts, move-validator.ts, llm.ts, move-prompt.ts
Update all imports in copied AI files: replace @/lib/game/gameLogic with ../../lib/game/gameLogic, replace @/types/game with ../../lib/game/types
AiService.generateMove() invokes moveGraph.invoke({ board }), validates result with isValidMove() defense-in-depth check, returns position
Add AI_AWS_BEDROCK_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AI_AWS_BEDROCK_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AI_AWS_BEDROCK_REGION to apps/tictactoe-api/.env.local and .env.example
AiModule imported by GameModule
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source files: apps/tictactoe/src/lib/ai/moveGraph.ts, nodes.ts, graphState.ts, moveValidator.ts, llm.ts, movePrompt.ts. Zod v4 .refine() second arg must be string or { message: string }, not function. DeepSeek-R1 uses <think>...</think> tags. llm.ts maxTokens is 2048.

userStories.title: AI move integration in make-move endpoint
userStories.description: As a player in AI mode, after I submit my move the backend should automatically trigger the AI pipeline and include the AI counter-move in the response.
acceptanceCriteria: In GameService.makeMove(): after applying human move in AI mode, if game is still in_progress, call AiService.generateMove(newBoard)
AI move is applied to the board via applyMove(board, aiPosition, 'O')
AI move recorded in moves array with correct moveNumber
Game status rechecked after AI move — update status/winner if AI wins or draws
currentTurn flips back to X after AI move (or stays on final status if game ends)
Response includes both human and AI moves in the moves array
Returns 500 with descriptive error if AI pipeline fails after retries
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Inject AiService into GameService. AI always plays O. The response bundles both moves so frontend gets complete state in one round trip.

userStories.title: PvP player token validation in make-move endpoint
userStories.description: As the backend, I need to validate that the player making a move in PvP mode is the correct player for the current turn.
acceptanceCriteria: In PvP mode, POST /api/games/:id/move requires X-Player-Token header
If currentTurn is X, token must match game's playerXToken
If currentTurn is O, token must match game's playerOToken
Returns 403 Forbidden if token is missing or does not match
AI mode does not require token validation (human is always X)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read token from request headers: @Headers('x-player-token') in controller, pass to service. Add token param to makeMove() service method.

userStories.title: Frontend REST API client
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a fetch-based API client to replace direct game logic calls with backend REST calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/tictactoe/src/lib/api/gameApi.ts
Export functions: createGame(mode: 'ai' | 'pvp'), getGame(id: string), makeMove(gameId: string, position: number, playerToken?: string), joinGame(gameId: string), listGames(limit?: number)
Uses NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL env var (default http://localhost:3002)
Includes X-Player-Token header when playerToken is provided
Each function throws descriptive Error on non-ok responses (include status code and response text)
All functions return typed response data (GameStateDto or array)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Simple fetch wrappers. No Apollo Client — these are REST endpoints, not GraphQL. Add NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:3002 to apps/tictactoe/.env.local.

userStories.title: Unified game board page at /game/[id]
userStories.description: As a player, I want a /game/:id page that loads the game by ID, renders the board, and handles moves via the REST API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/tictactoe/src/app/game/[id]/page.tsx as 'use client' component
On mount: fetches game state via getGame(params.id) from gameApi
Renders GameBoard, GameStatus, NewGameButton components using fetched game state
On cell click: calls makeMove(gameId, position, playerToken) from gameApi, updates local state from response
In AI mode: shows isAiThinking spinner while makeMove request is in-flight (response includes AI counter-move)
Reads playerToken from localStorage keyed by gameId
Handles error states: game not found (404), invalid move (400 with message), server error (500)
Displays error message to user on failed moves without crashing
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use existing GameBoard, GameStatus, NewGameButton, Cell components. State shape similar to current page.tsx but driven by API responses instead of local game logic. localStorage key format: playerToken-{gameId}.

userStories.title: AI mode landing page refactor
userStories.description: As a player visiting /, I want to immediately start a game against the AI.
acceptanceCriteria: Refactor apps/tictactoe/src/app/page.tsx: on load calls createGame('ai') from gameApi
Stores returned playerToken in localStorage keyed by gameId
Redirects to /game/{id} using next/navigation router.push()
Game history sidebar remains visible (uses listGames() from gameApi or existing useGameHistory hook)
Shows loading state while creating game
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This replaces the current page.tsx which manages game state locally. The new version is much simpler — just creates a game and redirects.

userStories.title: PvP Player X page at /x
userStories.description: As Player X, I want to visit /x to create a new PvP game and see a shareable link for Player O.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/tictactoe/src/app/x/page.tsx as 'use client' component
On load: calls createGame('pvp') from gameApi
Stores returned playerToken in localStorage keyed by gameId
Displays shareable link: /o?game={id} with copy-to-clipboard button
After showing link, navigates to /game/{id} (or renders board inline)
Shows loading state while creating game
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use navigator.clipboard.writeText() for copy. Show a 'Copied!' confirmation briefly.

userStories.title: PvP Player O page at /o
userStories.description: As Player O, I want to visit /o?game={id} to join an existing PvP game.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/tictactoe/src/app/o/page.tsx as 'use client' component
Reads game query param from URL using useSearchParams()
Calls joinGame(gameId) from gameApi
Stores returned playerToken in localStorage keyed by gameId
Redirects to /game/{id} using router.push()
Shows error if no game param provided
Shows error if game not found or join fails (not PvP, already joined, etc.)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Wrap in Suspense boundary since useSearchParams() requires it in Next.js 14 app router.

userStories.title: Remove frontend AI pipeline and old game logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to remove the AI pipeline and direct game logic from the frontend since the backend is now the source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete apps/tictactoe/src/app/api/ai-move/route.ts
Delete apps/tictactoe/src/lib/ai/ directory (all 6 files: moveGraph.ts, nodes.ts, graphState.ts, moveValidator.ts, llm.ts, movePrompt.ts)
Delete apps/tictactoe/src/lib/game/gameLogic.ts
Remove from frontend package.json: @langchain/aws, @langchain/core, @langchain/langgraph, zod
Add socket.io-client to frontend package.json
Update or remove any broken imports referencing deleted files
pnpm install succeeds
Typecheck passes
App builds successfully: pnpm build (from apps/tictactoe)
userStories.notes: Only safe to do after US-012 and US-013 are complete (new pages don't reference old game logic or AI pipeline). Check for any remaining imports of @/lib/ai/ or @/lib/game/gameLogic.

userStories.title: GameStatus component mode support
userStories.description: As a player, I want the game status display to adapt based on game mode (AI vs PvP).
acceptanceCriteria: GameStatus component accepts mode prop ('ai' | 'pvp') and optional playerSide prop ('X' | 'O')
AI mode messages: 'Your turn (X)' / 'AI is thinking...' (animate-pulse) / 'You win!' / 'AI wins!' / 'It is a draw!'
PvP mode messages: 'X's turn' / 'O's turn' / 'X wins!' / 'O wins!' / 'It is a draw!'
Existing component interface remains backward-compatible (mode defaults to 'ai')
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/tictactoe/src/components/GameStatus.tsx. Add mode and playerSide to the props interface. Keep the Record<GameStatus, ...> pattern but branch on mode.

userStories.title: Add Redis to Docker infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Redis running locally via Docker so the Socket.IO adapter can use it for horizontal scaling.
acceptanceCriteria: Add redis service to apps/tictactoe-api/docker-compose.yml: image redis:7-alpine, container_name tictactoe-redis, port mapping 6379:6379, restart unless-stopped
Add REDIS_HOST=localhost and REDIS_PORT=6379 to apps/tictactoe-api/.env.local and .env.example
docker-compose up -d starts both PostgreSQL and Redis containers
Redis accessible at localhost:6379
userStories.notes: Simple docker-compose addition. No application code changes — just infra. Redis alpine image is ~30MB.

userStories.title: WebSocket gateway for PvP real-time updates
userStories.description: As a PvP player, I want to see my opponent's moves in real-time without refreshing the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Install in tictactoe-api: @nestjs/websockets, @nestjs/platform-socket.io, socket.io, @socket.io/redis-adapter, ioredis
Create apps/tictactoe-api/src/modules/game/game.gateway.ts with @WebSocketGateway({ namespace: '/game', cors: { origin: '*' } })
Gateway configured with Redis adapter using REDIS_HOST and REDIS_PORT env vars
Gateway handles joinGame event: client joins Socket.IO room game-{gameId}
Gateway exposes broadcastGameUpdate(gameId, state) method
GameService calls gateway.broadcastGameUpdate() after each successful PvP move
Server emits gameUpdate event with GameStateDto to the game room
GameGateway registered as provider in GameModule
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: NestJS 8 @WebSocketGateway is stable. Use @WebSocketServer() decorator for Server instance. Redis adapter: createAdapter(ioredis pub, ioredis sub). Inject GameGateway into GameService.

userStories.title: Frontend WebSocket hook for PvP
userStories.description: As a PvP player, my game page should connect to WebSocket and update the board when my opponent moves.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/tictactoe/src/lib/api/useGameSocket.ts React hook
Hook accepts gameId (string | null), mode ('ai' | 'pvp'), and onUpdate callback
Connects to NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL /game namespace only when mode is pvp and gameId is provided
Emits joinGame { gameId } on connect
Listens for gameUpdate events and calls onUpdate with new GameStateDto
Cleans up socket connection on unmount or when gameId changes
Does nothing when mode is ai (no socket connection)
Update /game/[id]/page.tsx to use useGameSocket in PvP mode — onUpdate replaces local game state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open two tabs with same PvP game, make move in one, verify other updates
userStories.notes: Use socket.io-client: io(url + '/game', { transports: ['websocket'] }). Store socket in useRef. Cleanup in useEffect return.

userStories.title: PostGraphile read-only security via PostgreSQL roles
userStories.description: As a developer, I need PostGraphile restricted to read-only access so clients cannot bypass GameService by calling auto-generated mutations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new TypeORM migration that: creates postgraphile_user role with LOGIN and password, grants SELECT on game table, revokes INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on game table
Update PostGraphile config (apps/tictactoe-api/src/config/graphile.ts) to connect with postgraphile_user credentials
TypeORM connection continues using postgres superuser for full read/write via GameService
Add POSTGRAPHILE_USER and POSTGRAPHILE_PASSWORD to .env.local and .env.example
allGames GraphQL query still works (returns game data)
createGame / updateGameById GraphQL mutations return permission denied error
Migration runs successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Two DB connections: PostGraphile (read-only postgraphile_user role) and TypeORM (full-access postgres role). Migration SQL: CREATE ROLE postgraphile_user WITH LOGIN PASSWORD 'postgraphile'; GRANT SELECT ON game TO postgraphile_user;

userStories.title: Mode toggle UI
userStories.description: As a player, I want a UI toggle to switch between AI and PvP modes without manually editing the URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle or button group visible on the game pages: 'Play vs AI' / 'Play vs Human'
Selecting 'Play vs AI' navigates to / (creates new AI game)
Selecting 'Play vs Human' navigates to /x (creates new PvP game)
Current mode visually highlighted based on game state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Can be a simple component with two Button variants (default vs outline). Place above or below the game board in /game/[id]/page.tsx.

userStories.title: Game history sidebar with mode labels
userStories.description: As a player, I want the game history sidebar to show whether each game was AI or PvP.
acceptanceCriteria: GameHistoryItem component displays mode badge: 'AI' or 'PvP' using Badge component
Game history fetched via GET /api/games REST endpoint using listGames() from gameApi (replacing or supplementing PostGraphile query)
Mode badge uses secondary variant for AI, outline variant for PvP
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/tictactoe/src/components/GameHistoryItem.tsx. The REST endpoint returns mode field. May need to update useGameHistory hook or create a new hook that uses gameApi.listGames().
